QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_78

question.text: How do client-level position limits differ between equity derivatives and currency derivatives in India?

answer.text: Equity derivatives have position limits linked to MWPL (Market Wide Position Limit) of the underlying stock, while currency derivatives have gross position limits specified in USD notional terms
Both segments have identical position limits of 5% of total open interest
Currency derivatives have no client-level position limits since they are OTC-equivalent
Equity derivatives position limits are set by RBI while currency derivatives limits are set by SEBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In equity derivatives, client-level position limits for single-stock derivatives are linked to the MWPL (e.g., higher of Rs 500 crore or 15% of total OI). For currency derivatives, position limits are specified in notional USD terms (or equivalent) as per RBI/SEBI guidelines, reflecting the different nature of the underlying. **The Trap:** Position limits are NOT identical across segments — each segment has limits tailored to its market structure. Currency derivatives DO have client-level position limits. SEBI sets equity derivatives limits while currency derivatives limits involve both SEBI and RBI jointly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity position limits (MWPL framework), Series I covers currency position limits. Series XIII tests the comparative understanding across segments. Position limits for interest rate derivatives (Series IV) are based on notional value. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients trading across segments must track position limits separately for each segment. A breach in one segment does not affect limits in another, but the regulatory consequences are equally serious across all segments.
